---
title: "Time series at a coordinate"
subtitle: "Pull one voxel's series at a world-mm point."
jupyter: python3
sidebar: cookbook
---

## Goal

Open a 4-D BOLD and extract the time series at a world-millimetre
coordinate — with the affine inversion, rounding, and bounds check
behind a named operation.

## Code

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd().parents[1] / "src"))

import neuroim as ni

repo = Path.cwd().parents[1] if Path.cwd().name == "cookbook" else Path.cwd()
bold = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_bold.nii.gz"))

xyz = bold.space.grid_to_world((4, 4, 2))     # a point known to be in-grid
ts = bold.series_at_world(xyz)                # bare ndarray, length n_timepoints
typed = bold.series_roi_world(xyz)            # typed result with a Receipt

ts.shape, type(typed).__name__, typed.provenance.method_name
```

`series_at_world` is the array form; `series_roi_world` is the typed
form that carries provenance forward.

## What's guaranteed

- The affine inversion and rounding happen inside the call. There is
  no hand-written `np.linalg.inv(affine) @ [x, y, z, 1]`.
- A coordinate that maps off-grid raises before any indexing.

## What survives the file

`series_roi_world` returns an `ROIExtractionResult`. Its
`.provenance` is a `Receipt`; if you build a volume from it and
`to_nibabel()` it, the Receipt rides the NIfTI extension (see
[Save a derived map](save-derived-map-with-provenance.qmd)).

## Trapdoors

```{python}
#| error: true
bold.series_at_world((9999.0, 9999.0, 9999.0))
```

An off-grid world coordinate raises `ValueError`:
`world coord (…) mm maps to voxel (…) which is outside the image grid
of shape (…).` — *before* signed indexing can return a value from the
opposite corner. The `out_of_bounds="zero"` opt-in exists for
algorithms that intentionally want zero-fill; the default is `"raise"`.

## See also

- [Start 03 — ROI extraction](../start/03-roi.qmd)
- [Concept: Contract failures](../concepts/contract-failures.qmd) — the `WORLD-OOB` refusal.
- [Spec: contract-failure vocabulary](../spec/contract-failure-vocabulary.html)
